def transform(grid):
    n=10
    # copy grid
    g=[row[:] for row in grid]
    # identify horizontal runs len>1
    rows_runs=[]
    for r in range(n):
        c=0
        while c<n:
            if g[r][c]==7:
                start=c
                while c+1<n and g[r][c+1]==7:
                    c+=1
                end=c
                length=end-start+1
                if length>1:
                    rows_runs.append((r,start,end))
                c+=1
            else:
                c+=1
    # clear runs
    for r,start,end in rows_runs:
        for c in range(start,end+1):
            g[r][c]=0
    # place runs shifted right
    for r,start,end in rows_runs:
        length=end-start+1
        for i in range(length):
            g[r][n-1 - i]=7  # they may overlap? but simpler
        # ensure contiguous
        for i in range(n-length):
            pass
    # now process singles downward
    changed=True
    n=10
    while changed:
        changed=False
        for r in range(n-2,-1,-1):
            for c in range(n):
                if g[r][c]==7 and g[r+1][c]==0:
                    g[r+1][c]=7
                    g[r][c]=0
                    changed=True
    return g

from collections import deque
def transform_rule(grid):
    n=10
    visited=[[False]*n for _ in range(n)]
    output=[[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
    directions=[(1,0),(-1,0),(0,1),(0,-1)]
    for r in range(n):
        for c in range(n):
            if grid[r][c]==7 and not visited[r][c]:
                # bfs to get component
                q=deque([(r,c)])
                visited[r][c]=True
                cells=[]
                while q:
                    x,y=q.popleft()
                    cells.append((x,y))
                    for dx,dy in directions:
                        nx,ny=x+dx,y+dy
                        if 0<=nx<n and 0<=ny<n and grid[nx][ny]==7 and not visited[nx][ny]:
                            visited[nx][ny]=True
                            q.append((nx,ny))
                rows=[p[0] for p in cells]
                cols=[p[1] for p in cells]
                width=max(cols)-min(cols)+1
                height=max(rows)-min(rows)+1
                if width>height: # horizontal, push right
                    # compute relative positions row offset constant
                    # since horizontal line expected single row but maybe multi? We'll push each row separately?
                    # simpler: For each cell, we will produce new column = col + (n-1 - max(cols))
                    shift = n-1 - max(cols)
                    for x,y in cells:
                        output[x][y+shift]=7
                else: # vertical or square, push down
                    shift=n-1 - max(rows)
                    for x,y in cells:
                        output[x+shift][y]=7
    return output
```

def transform_rule2(grid):
    n=10
    visited=[[False]*n for _ in range(n)]
    output=[[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
    dirs=[(1,0),(-1,0),(0,1),(0,-1)]
    for r in range(n):
        for c in range(n):
            if grid[r][c]==7 and not visited[r][c]:
                # bfs component
                from collections import deque
                q=deque([(r,c)])
                visited[r][c]=True
                cells=[]
                while q:
                    x,y=q.popleft()
                    cells.append((x,y))
                    for dx,dy in dirs:
                        nx,ny=x+dx,y+dy
                        if 0<=nx<n and 0<=ny<n and grid[nx][ny]==7 and not visited[nx][ny]:
                            visited[nx][ny]=True
                            q.append((nx,ny))
                rows=[p[0] for p in cells]
                cols=[p[1] for p in cells]
                width=max(cols)-min(cols)+1
                height=max(rows)-min(rows)+1
                if width>height: # horizontal
                    # process row groups
                    row_map={}
                    for x,y in cells:
                        row_map.setdefault(x,[]).append(y)
                    for x, cols_list in row_map.items():
                        shift = n-1 - max(cols_list)
                        for y in cols_list:
                            output[x][y+shift]=7
                else: # vertical
                    col_map={}
                    for x,y in cells:
                        col_map.setdefault(y,[]).append(x)
                    for y, rows_list in col_map.items():
                        shift = n-1 - max(rows_list)
                        for x in rows_list:
                            output[x+shift][y]=7
    return output

def transform_rule_final(grid):
    n=10
    # classify cells horizontally oriented if part of horizontal run length>1
    horiz=[[False]*n for _ in range(n)]
    for r in range(n):
        c=0
        while c<n:
            if grid[r][c]==7:
                start=c
                while c+1<n and grid[r][c+1]==7:
                    c+=1
                end=c
                if end-start+1>1:  # horizontal run
                    for y in range(start,end+1):
                        horiz[r][y]=True
            c+=1
    output=[[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
    # process rows for horizontal
    for r in range(n):
        cols=[c for c in range(n) if horiz[r][c]]
        if cols:
            # preserve order (already sorted)
            length=len(cols)
            start_col=n-length
            for i,old_c in enumerate(cols):
                output[r][start_col+i]=7
    # process columns for vertical (cells not marked horiz)
    for c in range(n):
        rows=[r for r in range(n) if grid[r][c]==7 and not horiz[r][c]]
        if rows:
            num=len(rows)
            start_row=n-num
            for i in range(num):
                output[start_row+i][c]=7
    return output
```

def transform_rule(grid):

                cells=[]
                while q:

                    cells.append((x,y))
                    for dx,dy in directions:
                        nx,ny=x+dx,y+dy
                        if 0<=nx<n and 0<=ny<n and grid[nx][ny]==7 and not visited[nx][ny]:
                            visited[nx][ny]=True
                            q.append((nx,ny))
                rows=[p[0] for p in cells]
                cols=[p[1] for p in cells]
                width=max(cols)-min(cols)+1
                height=max(rows)-min(rows)+1
                if width>height: # horizontal, push right
                    # compute relative positions row offset constant
                    # since horizontal line expected single row but maybe multi? We'll push each row separately?
                    # simpler: For each cell, we will produce new column = col + (n-1 - max(cols))
                    shift = n-1 - max(cols)

                    shift=n-1 - max(rows)
                    for x,y in cells:

def transform_rule_final(grid):
    n=10
    # classify cells horizontally oriented if part of horizontal run length>1
    horiz=[[False]*n for _ in range(n)]
    for r in range(n):
        c=0
        while c<n:
            if grid[r][c]==7:
                start=c
                while c+1<n and grid[r][c+1]==7:
                    c+=1
                end=c
                if end-start+1>1:  # horizontal run
                    for y in range(start,end+1):
                        horiz[r][y]=True
            c+=1
    output=[[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
    # process rows for horizontal
    for r in range(n):
        cols=[c for c in range(n) if horiz[r][c]]
        if cols:
            # preserve order (already sorted)
            length=len(cols)
            start_col=n-length
            for i,old_c in enumerate(cols):
                output[r][start_col+i]=7
    # process columns for vertical (cells not marked horiz)
    for c in range(n):
        rows=[r for r in range(n) if grid[r][c]==7 and not horiz[r][c]]
        if rows:
            num=len(rows)
            start_row=n-num
            for i in range(num):
                output[start_row+i][c]=7
    return output

def components(grid):
    H=len(grid)
    W=len(grid[0])
    visited=[[False]*W for _ in range(H)]
    comps=[]
    for r in range(H):
        for c in range(W):
            if grid[r][c]==6 and not visited[r][c]:
                comp=[]
                queue=[(r,c)]
                visited[r][c]=True
                while queue:
                    rr,cc=queue.pop()
                    comp.append((rr,cc))
                    for dr,dc in [(1,0),(-1,0),(0,1),(0,-1)]:
                        nr,nc=rr+dr,cc+dc
                        if 0<=nr<H and 0<=nc<W and grid[nr][nc]==6 and not visited[nr][nc]:
                            visited[nr][nc]=True
                            queue.append((nr,nc))
                comps.append(comp)
    return comps
def shift_component(comp,dx):
    return [(r, c+dx) for r,c in comp]

def transform_comps(grid):
    H=len(grid); W=len(grid[0])
    comps=components(grid)
    # sort by smallest original leftmost column
    comps.sort(key=lambda comp: min(c for r,c in comp))
    out=[[0]*W for _ in range(H)]
    for comp in comps:
        # compute max shift left: start with dx = -min_col
        minc=min(c for r,c in comp)
        dx=-minc
        while True:
            shifted=shift_component(comp,dx)
            # if any out of bounds
            if any(c<0 for r,c in shifted):
                raise
            # if overlap with existing out grid
            if any(out[r][c]==6 for r,c in shifted):
                dx+=1
                continue
            else:
                for r,c in shifted:
                    out[r][c]=6
                break
    return out

def components(grid):
    H=len(grid)
    W=len(grid[0])
    visited=[[False]*W for _ in range(H)]
    comps=[]
    for r in range(H):
        for c in range(W):
            if grid[r][c]==6 and not visited[r][c]:
                comp=[]
                queue=[(r,c)]
                visited[r][c]=True
                while queue:
                    rr,cc=queue.pop()
                    comp.append((rr,cc))
                    for dr,dc in [(1,0),(-1,0),(0,1),(0,-1)]:
                        nr,nc=rr+dr,cc+dc
                        if 0<=nr<H and 0<=nc<W and grid[nr][nc]==6 and not visited[nr][nc]:
                            visited[nr][nc]=True
                            queue.append((nr,nc))
                comps.append(comp)
    return comps
def shift_component(comp,dx):
    return [(r, c+dx) for r,c in comp]

def transform_comps(grid):
    H=len(grid); W=len(grid[0])
    comps=components(grid)
    # sort by smallest original leftmost column
    comps.sort(key=lambda comp: min(c for r,c in comp))
    out=[[0]*W for _ in range(H)]
    for comp in comps:
        # compute max shift left: start with dx = -min_col
        minc=min(c for r,c in comp)
        dx=-minc
        while True:
            shifted=shift_component(comp,dx)
            # if any out of bounds
            if any(c<0 for r,c in shifted):
                raise
            # if overlap with existing out grid
            if any(out[r][c]==6 for r,c in shifted):
                dx+=1
                continue
            else:
                for r,c in shifted:
                    out[r][c]=6
                break
    return out

```python
def shift_component(comp, delta):
    return {(r, c-delta) for r,c in comp}
def left_shift_amount(comp, occupied):
    # compute maximum delta such that shifted comp not collide and not go negative
    delta = min(c for r,c in comp)  # cannot shift more than this to keep >=0
    for d in range(delta, -1, -1):
        shifted = {(r,c - d) for r,c in comp}
        if not shifted & occupied:
            return d
    return 0
def transform(grid):
    comps=get_components(grid)
    # sort comps by min column ascending
    comps_sorted=sorted(comps, key=lambda comp:min(c for r,c in comp))
    occupied=set()
    for comp in comps_sorted:
        d=left_shift_amount(comp, occupied)
        shifted=shift_component(comp,d)
        occupied |= shifted
    # build new grid
    new=[[0]*10 for _ in range(10)]
    for r,c in occupied:
        new[r][c]=6
    return new
